column36: Another great setup. The only hero I had played before was Quake and I didn't love her then. After playing this setup I can see some of her strengths.  I tried setting up one deck for dark memories and the other for focus.

Lost first game, second one was on 15 heroes in KO, and knew next twist is loss. Also developed lots of negative feelings on Scarlet Witch hero set, it was so nice to see hew cards going to KO from twists.
I love playing with SHIELD Level.  I picked a SHIELD Hero and a FF Hero so each player could focus on one or the other.  The SHIELD player rocked while the FF player just kind of trickled along.

Setup was a lot of fun. I leaned into the Dark Memories this time. That definitely helped with the Cosmic Threat villains along with outpacing Hood's Gang. Biggest spook of the night was when Morg cleared my HQ and revealed 3 rares in one go. That ended up helping a lot, but I was worried I wouldn't be able to afford even 2 of the 3 before they were KOed.  Not a huge fan of this scheme, which is worded a little confusing. Twists 5-8 are really just board wipes and if you didn't control your HQ for the first four twists, the game was going to end before you get to twist 7 or 8. My first game didn't go well, but the 2nd was a lot of fun. I choose Deathlok to try to outsmart the scheme (removing Shield heroes from the KO pile) and chose Black Widow to give him some Tech support. But I really only got to do what I wanted once. Even though my trick didn't work, the two heroes helped out because they're strong heroes. Also I have a new appreciation for The Thing. Overall, lots of fun.

This was a tough setup. Got lucky in the second attempt that I only KO'd 2 heroes through the first 4 twists. The 4 undercovers for player 1 made Quake a monster especially with two Tectonic Wave cards. The best way I found to counter dark memories for The Hood is to have dark memories cards in your hand to cancel out the effect.

This was one of the hardest and best setup's I've played. I thought all the heros worked well together and the mastermind was one I hadn't played against. The first game the scheme really got to me quickly. I thought I was doing well and then went on a run of twist and it was just too much to handle. Second game ran very similar but got luck the twist didn't come as quickly.

Eurgh.  So much calculating shifting attack.  

I was waffling a little on Hero choice, but I think I chose right for me.  My first play through worked just like I wanted it to.  So I kept the Heroes and played a second game, and it went much, much worse.  I'm thankful it went in that order, because if the bad game happened first I probably would have changed Heroes and had a not as good game to submit.

I'm always stoked to play a game with Howard the Duck, and this provided such a perfect opportunity for him to pop off! Lots of bystanders for his commons to rescue, and Janet Van Dyne and the Freedom Fighters made it insanely easy to pop off. I got Klaw locked into an Instinct frequency which made him easy to hit. I Ended up choosing to take an extra turn instead of whapping Klaw on turn 25 to generate even more PPT, hoping it works out for me.

Throw in some general Okoye to deal with wounds and this is a fun group of heroes together.

column36: This game was crazy. Ebony Maw ascended, Reverend Achebe turned into an Evolved Ultron, Klaw helped me land the Final Blow on a huge last turn that would’ve otherwise been 1 attack short of hitting him for the second time, narrowly avoided multiple escapes. Great job on the setup, it was fantastic. Both sides utilized HQ manipulation to keep Klaw low. And Jentorra’s “Conquer the Conquerer” is an absolute house (especially with those extra Ultron villains) that enabled 2 huge turns to really help me finish off Klaw. Honestly, peak Legendary.
I liked the constant HQ cycling, but Age of Ultron scheme is unreasonably difficult.

This one ended up being a bit unlucky for us. I was using prof. X to try and play off of ice man, who is crazy strong, but could not get icemans cards to come up at all in either game. In both rounds we hit the last twist, but game two we hit all 6 before turn 25. My wife's deck ended up a little unfocused, but she was really getting there when game two ended. Apocalypse is not our fave because of his"if this was not the last tactic..." But it second round we were much more economic in our purchase patterns. Black widow is so broken lol

It's never easy to fight Apocalypse specially with a time limit scheme, I tried to use angel and cyclops to get high attack with few cards but I kept losing good heroes by master strikes or death. Not such a fun game to play and having to play almost a core game on the second week worries me how it will be the next set ups when we are combined by points.
Annoyingly hard, but worse it was just not interesting. Appreciate all the hard work you put into this tourney and these setups, but this one was a dud.

This setup is deceptively more difficult than we expected. Lost both of our attempts despite there being a pretty hefty amount of cards left in the villain deck. Both games started with a scheme twist right off the bat. It was a struggle to try and build decks with key pieces getting ko'd from the random scheme twists. Both games had multiple times where a hero's rare would enter the HQ just to immediately be ko'd from a twist. Double Wolverine is always a strong combo and being able to quickly and cheaply pick up a functional deck was good for both games. The other player was a cap/rogue deck for game one just to try and copy and stack colors, but all of the copy rogues got ko'd from twists. The second game was cap/invisible woman to try and capitalize and build a bunch of colors and 4 costs. Apocalypse is always a tough matchup despite being from such an old set, and his master strike essentially floating through two turns in a row definitely hits hard. Both games we hit the tactic that shuffles back in which also extends the game. We were definitely blindsided by the difficulty. We didn't really enjoy playing with such limited sets, especially since we are (almost) collection complete. No sidekicks, special shield officers, more special bystanders, etc. is definitely noticeable when you strip them all away after playing with them all for so long. Overall upset by our performance.
I enjoy playing against Apocalypse...but he is still Immortal and Undefeated.

We knew we were in for a tough match when we saw the setup, and yeah it lived up to it. We thought Bishop + Invisible Woman would give us the quickest path to power to attack Apocalypse before the scheme killed us. Our decks started humming as we were able to thin them out, but unfortunately Apocalypse saved his Master Strikes for the most inopportune time for us. He spaced them out towards the end of the match in such a way that we pretty much lost 4 turns in a row, and the scheme was ultimately fulfilled... Tough cookies!

Seemed like a race to infinite. I knew I needed dodge so black widow came along. And sabretooth allows a good amount of top deck manipulation, including discarding, that complemented the infinite chase based on instinct cards. Both hands were racing towards dodges, but the left hand handled enough multiple men to thin the deck a lot and ended up nearly entirely instinct. Sabretooths instinct cards manipulated the top of the deck so the dodging would work faster. Good synergies, good stuff, all good things.

Bastion is always a difficult run here. We tried a few things in the end to try and get a good placement. Opting to go for a Shield Plan and to take advantage of the X-Force with Colossus and Cable. Using Cable's ability to get a +2 against Masterminds really helped to spread Bastion's flood of sentinel masterminds. 

Our first run was a little slow, ending with 33 rounds with Agent Phil Coulson feeling a bit awkward in hand. 

In Game 2, swapping Coulson for Deathlok made our hand feel a bit stronger. We were able to get our gray cards out quickly throwing them undercover and basically draw out our deck every turn to constantly get 17+ attack to ravage the field. Might have been the best run ever done against this Mastermind.

column36: Beginning was a bit tight with many villains (feat Clone) arriving without any scheme twists. Just avoided getting escapes. Then in early-mid game clan yashida took over 3/5 of the HQ. After getting some good card combos (and getting to shield level 2 finally) everything became more easy and turbulent. By turn 26 the Mockingbird-Coulson deck was quite ready, and with 27 attack Mockingbird took down 4 different Bastion iterations. On next turn colussus finished the job. (I was somewhat lucky as I have avoided most of the master strikes, and Bastions only got +2 power, so it was quite managable.) Fun mechanics all around. Spider-woman and Deathlok did not see much play, but that happens.
Final two turns featured rares from Daredevil, Mockingbird, and M and over 50 Attack across the two turns. Went from a stable mid-game to done in 2 turns.

column36:
"We can still join the tournament on vacation, he says.... We can play online, it will be fine, he says..." 1am the night before the deadline trying to cram in the game playing on TTS for our first time... :P 
Know we would have done better with plotting out vs. the scheme at home but at least have a final result to turn in.

Interesting setup. I went with Wolfsbane to try to mesh with Mirage on Moonlight, and to get more deck manipulation. Doctor Strange Supreme was more deck manipulation and KO, and I thought Soulbind might be useful for the zombies. These heroes were also attack heavy. My plan was just to kill all zombies immediately to avoid Twist escapes and go after the somewhat weak mastermind.

Game 1 went really well. I got some decent attack and thinned out my decks quickly. Got a little lucky that I only saw 1 of the Twists. I was underwhelmed by Soulbind though. I ended up with Doctor Strange's rare, and even though I played it 3 or 4 times I think the most draw I got out of it was 2. Moonlight was basically free, so Wolfsbane really started carrying things. The Piercing Energy from fighting Phoenix was also a nice surprise, and helped clear the city.

Game 2 was bad. In the first handful of turns I had already watched a Phoenix Zombie escape with a Twist and I had collected 2 wounds on each hand from Strikes. Took me 30 turns to win that game, and it felt slow the whole time.

very nice set-up! went smooth for me! ( i played the marvel zombies scheme with a friend about 2 weeks ago and it just so happens we also used jotunheim giants in that set-up so i had kind of a good practice round already i guess XD )

Super fun setup, we both enjoyed this one a lot. Slower than our first game, but we're confident in it. Fighting the Phoenix rare while running a berserk deck was terrifying, but worked out. (we put Phoenix in the Other card category)

My choices for this week were disappointing in their results, but that was due to an overestimation on my part of the number of cards that could possibly help. I chose Apocalyptic Black WIdow and Killmonger Spec-Ops, mostly because they could get bystanders behind villain cards to keep the zombies buried. However, since these cards are one uncommon and 1 common with MCU distribution, there were only 5 in the entire hero deck, so not likely to show up and that's exactly what happened.

The setup had plenty of easily gained bystanders as human shields though. When things started rolling in attempt 1 I wasn't all that worried anymore. Arcade was not that hard to defeat and keeping the zombies managed went well farther into the game. 1 early escape was not to be avoided. Attempt 2 had 2 escapes, which weren't really avoidable. Both games it was due to a twist, not because the CIty was full.

In game 1 Mirage and Party Thor carried the most, with a deck 1 that was mostly their cards. Deck 2 was more diverse, but also less succesful. Attempt 2 was clearly a slower game, as the first Mastermind hit was in turn 19, when attempt 1 was already over, but then picked up pace to end in 25 turns. But Attempt 1 also had 1 fewer escape, so I'm submitting that one.

Giants of Jotnuheim are KO henchmen, albeit a Henchman group whoses KO is harder to use. I found the total setup fair and interesting. It wasn't too brutal, but it also was no cakewalk. A lot depends on how early twists show up. Looking forward to next week's challenge!

P1 was mostly X-23 with some other Instinct cards, managed to get enough KOs to churn out consistent Attack, especially with help from the Jotunheim Giants to activate X-Gene. Discarding some Empathic Links and then copying them with Prodigy helped get enough to buy Legion’s rare for that deck next turn. P2 got some Party Thor cards and just bought all the expensive cards getting nice attack bonuses for doing so. First game was also a blast, one of the best showings I’ve seen from X-23’s rare card, but alas too many zombie escapes early on and I lost before I could the final hit.

Wow...this was a really fun setup. Lots of early tension and late game battles. Was able to get some good synergies going, but felt like things took a bit to get there. Second attempt was a disaster (as I was just hammered by Rise as well as Wounds). Again, this setup felt so unique to Legendary gameplay...something I really love about this game. Thanks!
used Widow and killmonger to try and hand out bystanders to bury the zombies, and it just never came up ,sigh, best laid plans and all that, still won with a relatively quick game
Great choices for this pod! It was a blast! Almost got us but we did it. Go us! Twist came out early so it was great!
It was fun but it found it hard to progress (i.e hit the MM)
